revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:兵主神社 (岸和田市)]] {{Infobox religious building |名称 = 兵主神社 |image = File:Hyozujinjya Haiden, Kishiwada.jpg |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]西之内町1-1 |位置 = |ISO = JP-27 | 緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 28 |緯度秒 = 14.8 | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 23 |経度秒 = 29.4 |ラベル位置 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神 = [[天照皇大神]]
[[八幡大神]]
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]] |別名 = 大宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |deity = * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Hachiman]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|Chiyou|lt=Hyozu-no-Kami|ja|兵主神|en|Chiyou|ru|Чи Ю|fr|Chiyou|es|Chi You|zh|蚩尤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyou|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |prefecture = {{ill|Kishiwada|qid=Q740456|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.470778|N|135.3915|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Hyozu Shrine''' (兵主神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|qid=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|Engishiki shrine|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a shrine of {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}}. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} and is also known as "Ōmiya," which influenced the naming of {{ill|Izumi-Ōmiya Station|en|Izumi-Ōmiya Station|qid=Q6102276|lt=Izumi-Ōmiya Station|1=Izumi-Ōmiya Station}}. == History == Hyozu Shrine served as the general shrine (sōja) for the twelve villages of Kamoru District. During the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1593), a fire destroyed many historical records, leaving the founding date unknown. However, the shrine appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} among the 28 shrines of Izumi Province’s {{ill|Izumi District|ja|和泉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi District|1=Izumi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10982247}}. The main hall was rebuilt by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|qid=Q319531|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}. An ancient document from {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|qid=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 6 (1666) states that "Noh performances continued for 62 years," suggesting that performances were held until {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|qid=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku|1=Eiroku}} 10 (1567). Nine existing Noh masks, known as "Masks of Divine Descent," were used in these performances. In {{ill|Kanpō|en|Kanpō|qid=Q1145798|lt=Kanpō|1=Kanpō}} 3 (1743), the lord of the {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|en|Kishiwada Domain|qid=Q3034469|lt=Kishiwada Domain|1=Kishiwada Domain}} donated a paulownia wood box and brocade mask pouches. In {{ill|1873|en|1873|qid=Q7757|lt=Meiji 6|1=1873}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities - {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q261637}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}, and {{ill|Chiyou|lt=Hyozu-no-Kami|ja|兵主神|en|Chiyou|ru|Чи Ю|fr|Chiyou|es|Chi You|zh|蚩尤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyou|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Grounds == * '''Main hall''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt by Toyotomi Hideyoshi during the Azuchi-Momoyama period. It features a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a front gable, covered with {{ill|hiwada-buki|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hiwada-buki|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q90373477}}. * '''Worship hall''' * '''Shrine office''' * '''Gūō Shrine''' * '''Ryūō Shrine''' * '''Thirteen-tier stone pagoda''' * '''Ōmiya Inari Shrine''' * '''Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine''' * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' [[File:Hyozujinjya Honden, Kishiwada.jpg|thumb|Main hall]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * '''Main Hall''' - Built in the Azuchi-Momoyama period, three-bay nagare-zukuri with a front gable and hiwada-buki roofing. Designated on April 15, 1924.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/122954|title=Hyozu Shrine Main Hall|publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency|accessdate=2020-1-26}} === Kishiwada City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Noh Masks''' - Includes one paper mask, nine mask pouches, one wooden box, and a donation record. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * '''Hyozu Shrine Sacred Grove''' == Festivals == * Sai-tan Festival * Ebisu Festival * Inari Festival * Spring Festival * Summer Festival * Gūjin Festival * Autumn Festival ** {{ill|Kishiwada Danjiri Festival|ja|岸和田十月祭礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishiwada Danjiri Festival|qq=}} * Winter Festival == Visitor Information == * '''Location''' ** 1-1 Nishinochi-chō, Kishiwada, Osaka Prefecture * '''Access''' ** 15-minute walk from {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|qid=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line|1=Nankai Main Line}} {{ill|Izumi-Ōmiya Station|en|Izumi-Ōmiya Station|qid=Q6102276|lt=Izumi-Ōmiya Station|1=Izumi-Ōmiya Station}}. * '''Parking''' ** Available == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Hyōzu Shrine|ja|兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōzu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Important Cultural Properties of Japan (Buildings and structures in Kinki)|ja|近畿地方にある建造物の Important Cultural Properties一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Important Cultural Properties of Japan (Buildings and structures in Kinki)|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Official website|https://hyouzu.jp/}} * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/soshiki/36/hyouzujinja.html Hyozu Shrine – Kishiwada City] * {{National Heritage Database|102|2146|Hyozu Shrine Main Hall}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto navbox}}
